question_title: What will be the value of "30-Day Average SOFR" on 2026-03-26?
summary: The 30-Day Average SOFR on March 26, 2026 is highly constrained by two factors: (1) 16 of 30 calendar days in the averaging window already have fixed SOFR values, and (2) the FOMC meeting on March 17-18 has 92%+ probability of holding rates at 3.50-3.75%. The distribution is bimodal: ~92% probability tightly clustered around 3.660-3.668 (hold scenario), and ~8% probability around 3.594-3.601 (25bp cut scenario). Monte Carlo simulation with 500K runs using verified compound interest methodology produces a median of ~3.664. The window for March 26 (Feb 25 - Mar 26) excludes the Feb 17-18 SOFR spike, mechanically pulling the average down from the current 3.672 to approximately 3.663. All P10-P90 percentiles fall within the hold cluster since the cut probability (~8%) is below the P10 threshold.

factors.description: Current SOFR30DAYAVG is 3.67223 (Mar 13). The Mar 26 window shifts forward, dropping the Feb 12-24 period (which included the Feb 17-18 spike of 3.71-3.73) and adding Mar 13-26 days at ~3.65. This mechanically pulls the average down to ~3.663.
FOMC meeting Mar 17-18 with 92%+ probability of rate hold at 3.50-3.75%. Daily SOFR will remain pinned near 3.64-3.65 in hold scenario, keeping the 30-day average stable.
~8% probability of 25bp rate cut at FOMC meeting. If cut occurs, daily SOFR drops to ~3.40, pulling the 30-day average down to ~3.597 (only 8 of 14 future calendar days affected).

16 of 30 calendar days in the window are already fixed with known SOFR values. This severely constrains the possible range of the 30-day average, making the distribution very tight in the hold scenario (std ~0.002).
Possible minor quarter-end effects as Mar 26 is 5 days before quarter-end. Pre-quarter-end SOFR could be slightly elevated on Mar 25-26, adding small upward bias.

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**30-Day Average SOFR on March 26, 2026**

The forecast is tightly constrained by two key facts:

1. **53% of the averaging window is already fixed** — 16 of 30 calendar days have known SOFR values
2. **FOMC holds with 92%+ probability** — Daily SOFR will stay pinned near 3.64-3.65%

The 30-day average will decline from the current 3.672% because the March 26 window (Feb 25 – Mar 26) no longer includes the Feb 17-18 SOFR spike (3.71-3.73%), which elevated the current reading.

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 3.6596 |
| P20 | 3.6615 |
| P40 | 3.6631 |
| P60 | 3.6642 |
| P80 | 3.6656 |
| P90 | 3.6665 |

The distribution is extremely tight in the dominant hold scenario (std ~0.002), with a small ~8% tail risk from a potential rate cut that would pull the average down to ~3.60. The Monte Carlo simulation (500K runs) was verified against published SOFR30DAYAVG values using the NY Fed's compound interest methodology.
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series SOFR30DAYAVG once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=SOFR30DAYAVG`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-03-26 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
